• Show All Code
  • Hide All Code
  • Download Rmd

Correlation of RNA-seq and CUT&RUN results

Libraries

library(tidyverse)
library(plyranges)

Functions

Read RNA

res_housing_cs_combat <- read_tsv("~/repos/rnaseq_social_isolation/data_output/combat/res_housing_cs_combat.tsv")
Rows: 13338 Columns: 8
── Column specification ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Delimiter: "\t"
chr (3): geneid, gene_symbol, chr
dbl (5): baseMean, log2FoldChange, lfcSE, pvalue, padj

ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data.
ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.
library(org.Dm.eg.db)
library(GenomicFeatures)
library(TxDb.Dmelanogaster.UCSC.dm6.ensGene)


gene_id_map <- clusterProfiler::bitr(fromType = "ENSEMBL",toType = "SYMBOL",geneID = res_housing_cs_combat$geneid,OrgDb = org.Dm.eg.db)
'select()' returned 1:many mapping between keys and columns
Warning in clusterProfiler::bitr(fromType = "ENSEMBL", toType = "SYMBOL",  :
  1.27% of input gene IDs are fail to map...
res_housing_cs_combat <- res_housing_cs_combat[res_housing_cs_combat$geneid %in% gene_id_map$ENSEMBL,]

res_housing_cs_combat <- left_join(res_housing_cs_combat,gene_id_map,by = c("geneid" = "SYMBOL"))

promoters_dm6 <- promoters(genes(TxDb.Dmelanogaster.UCSC.dm6.ensGene),upstream = 1,downstream = 1)
  1 gene was dropped because it has exons located on both strands of the same
  reference sequence or on more than one reference sequence, so cannot be
  represented by a single genomic range.
  Use 'single.strand.genes.only=FALSE' to get all the genes in a GRangesList
  object, or use suppressMessages() to suppress this message.
Warning in valid.GenomicRanges.seqinfo(x, suggest.trim = TRUE) :
  GRanges object contains 3 out-of-bound ranges located on sequences
  chrY_DS484142v1_random, chrY_DS484909v1_random, and chrY_DS485523v1_random.
  Note that ranges located on a sequence whose length is unknown (NA) or on a
  circular sequence are not considered out-of-bound (use seqlengths() and
  isCircular() to get the lengths and circularity flags of the underlying
  sequences). You can use trim() to trim these ranges. See
  ?`trim,GenomicRanges-method` for more information.
res_housing_cs_combat <- res_housing_cs_combat[res_housing_cs_combat$geneid %in% names(promoters_dm6),]


promoters_dm6 <- promoters_dm6[res_housing_cs_combat$geneid]

elementMetadata(promoters_dm6) <- DataFrame(res_housing_cs_combat)

Read CUT&RUN

cutnrun_h3k27ac <- makeGRangesFromDataFrame(readRDS("../data_output/diffbind_cutnrun/results_cutnrun_H3K27ac_macs2_peaks_union_deseq_ashr.tsv"),keep.extra.columns = TRUE)

cutnrun_h3k4me3 <-  makeGRangesFromDataFrame(readRDS("../data_output/diffbind_cutnrun/results_cutnrun_H3K4me3_macs2_peaks_union_deseq_ashr.tsv") ,keep.extra.columns = TRUE)
cutnrun_igg <-  makeGRangesFromDataFrame(readRDS("../data_output/diffbind_cutnrun/results_cutnrun_IgG_macs2_peaks_union_deseq_ashr.tsv"),keep.extra.columns = TRUE)
cutnrun_rnapolii <-  makeGRangesFromDataFrame(readRDS("../data_output/diffbind_cutnrun/results_cutnrun_RNAPolII_macs2_peaks_union_deseq_ashr.tsv"),keep.extra.columns = TRUE)

Join tables

seqlevels(promoters_dm6) <- str_remove(seqlevels(promoters_dm6),"chr")
colnames(elementMetadata(promoters_dm6)) <- paste0(colnames(elementMetadata(promoters_dm6)),"_rnaseq")

cutnrun_h3k27ac_rna <- plyranges::join_overlap_left_within(promoters_dm6,cutnrun_h3k27ac)
Warning in .Seqinfo.mergexy(x, y) :
  Each of the 2 combined objects has sequence levels not in the other:
  - in 'x': M, X_CP007103v1_random, X_CP007104v1_random, X_DS483648v1_random, X_DS483655v1_random, X_DS483660v1_random, X_DS483665v1_random, X_DS483666v1_random, X_DS483669v1_random, X_DS483685v1_random, X_DS483698v1_random, X_DS483745v1_random, X_DS483784v1_random, X_DS483789v1_random, X_DS483795v1_random, X_DS483803v1_random, X_DS483809v1_random, X_DS483818v1_random, X_DS483821v1_random, X_DS483843v1_random, X_DS483851v1_random, X_DS483885v1_random, X_DS483888v1_random, X_DS483892v1_random, X_DS483893v1_random, X_DS483897v1_random, X_DS483903v1_random, X_DS483905v1_random, X_DS483907v1_random, X_DS483909v1_random, X_DS483923v1_random, X_DS483926v1_random, X_DS483928v1_random, X_DS483946v1_random, X_DS483948v1_random, X_DS483950v1_random, X_DS483955v1_random, X_DS483963v1_random, X_DS483969v1_random, X_DS483971v1_random, X_DS483974v1_random, X_DS483995v1_random, X_DS484002v1_random, X_DS484005v1_random, X_DS484012v1_ [... truncated]

Plots


options(scipen = 999)
as.data.frame(cutnrun_h3k27ac_rna) %>%
  ggplot(aes(log2FoldChange_rnaseq, log2FoldChange)) +
  geom_point(size = 0.2) +
  ggpubr::stat_cor() +
  theme_light() +
  theme(aspect.ratio = 1) +
  ggtitle("H3K27ac")
Warning: Removed 3267 rows containing non-finite values (`stat_cor()`).
Warning: Removed 3267 rows containing missing values (`geom_point()`).

as.data.frame(cutnrun_h3k4me3_rna) %>%
  ggplot(aes(log2FoldChange_rnaseq, log2FoldChange)) +
  geom_point(size = 0.2) +
  ggpubr::stat_cor() +
  theme_light() +
  theme(aspect.ratio = 1) +
  ggtitle("H3K4me3")
Warning: Removed 4231 rows containing non-finite values (`stat_cor()`).
Warning: Removed 4231 rows containing missing values (`geom_point()`).

as.data.frame(cutnrun_igg_rna) %>%
  ggplot(aes(log2FoldChange_rnaseq, log2FoldChange)) +
  geom_point(size = 0.2) +
  ggpubr::stat_cor() +
  theme_light() +
  theme(aspect.ratio = 1) +
  ggtitle("IgG")
Warning: Removed 2141 rows containing non-finite values (`stat_cor()`).
Warning: Removed 2141 rows containing missing values (`geom_point()`).

as.data.frame(cutnrun_rnapolii_rna) %>%
  ggplot(aes(log2FoldChange_rnaseq, log2FoldChange)) +
  geom_point(size = 0.2) +
  ggpubr::stat_cor() +
  theme_light() +
  theme(aspect.ratio = 1) +
  ggtitle("RNAPolII")
Warning: Removed 2517 rows containing non-finite values (`stat_cor()`).
Warning: Removed 2517 rows containing missing values (`geom_point()`).

options(scipen = 999)
as.data.frame(cutnrun_h3k27ac_rna) %>%
  filter(padj_rnaseq < 0.05) %>%
  ggplot(aes(log2FoldChange_rnaseq, log2FoldChange)) +
  geom_point(size = 0.2) +
  ggpubr::stat_cor() +
  theme_light() +
  theme(aspect.ratio = 1) +
  ggtitle("H3K27ac")
Warning: Removed 61 rows containing non-finite values (`stat_cor()`).
Warning: Removed 61 rows containing missing values (`geom_point()`).

  geom_point(size = 0.2) +
  ggpubr::stat_cor() +
  theme_light() +

as.data.frame(cutnrun_rnapolii_rna) %>%
  filter(padj_rnaseq < 0.05) %>%
  ggplot(aes(log2FoldChange_rnaseq, log2FoldChange)) +
  geom_point(size = 0.2) +

Warning: Removed 41 rows containing non-finite values (`stat_cor()`).
Warning: Removed 41 rows containing missing values (`geom_point()`).

cutnrun_rnapolii %>%
  as_data_frame() %>%
  ggplot(aes(baseMean, log2FoldChange, color = padj < 0.05)) +
  geom_point() +
  scale_x_log10() +
  theme_light() +
  theme(aspect.ratio = 1) +
  ggtitle("RNAPolII")


cutnrun_h3k27ac %>%
  as_data_frame() %>%
  ggplot(aes(baseMean, log2FoldChange, color = padj < 0.05)) +
  geom_point() +
  scale_x_log10() +
  theme_light() +
  theme(aspect.ratio = 1) +
  ggtitle("H3K27ac")

  as_data_frame() %>%
  ggplot(aes(baseMean, log2FoldChange, color = padj < 0.05)) +
  geom_point() +
  scale_x_log10() +
  theme_light() +
  theme(aspect.ratio = 1) +
  ggtitle("H3K4me3")

  as_data_frame() %>%
  ggplot(aes(baseMean, log2FoldChange, color = padj < 0.05)) +
  geom_point() +
  scale_x_log10() +
  theme_light() +
  theme(aspect.ratio = 1) +
  ggtitle("IgG")
